explanation: Benign activity pattern: backup zip creation (23MB), office file reads with zero deltas, temp file creation. No extension changes, no suspicious processes, no registry tampering, no Defender alerts. Low observability but all visible events are consistent with normal backup/office operations.

Active blitz encryption in progress. Windows Defender disabled via registry (DisableAntiSpyware) with Event ID 5001 across multiple time windows. Current window shows 6+ files being actively encrypted with .encrypted extension changes across Documents, Desktop, ProgramData, and Temp. Prior windows show .enc extensions appearing. High entropy confirmed on encrypted files. Immediate blocking required to prevent further encryption.

Massive blitz ransomware attack in progress at 37% completion. Windows Defender disabled via registry (DisableAntiSpyware) with Event ID 5001 across all time windows. Attack progression: t-2 showed 3 .locked files, t-1 escalated to 11+ .locked files, current window shows 15+ files with .encrypted extensions. Attack has spread across Documents, Desktop, Pictures, Downloads, ProgramData, and Temp. Files confirm high entropy (7.5+). Immediate block required - encryption actively spreading.

Clearly benign activity. Windows Defender (MsMpEng.exe) running and completed a clean scan (Event ID 1001: 'No threats found'). Firefox browser downloading a PDF. Excel editing spreadsheets. Current window shows normal small file edits (size deltas under 600 bytes). No extension changes, no registry tampering, no suspicious processes. High observability (0.9) provides comprehensive view - all activity is routine office/browsing/AV work.
